commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=de1f5ea68be40b7b53472abbe706ee876613eb0f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway managed-document attachment pipeline, route, chat hooks, cleanup/promotion handling, tests, and a changelog entry for serving assistant-generated documents as downloads.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main docs define managed non-video attachment history behavior, while the chat path only creates managed image blocks and the image module skips non-image MIME types.
commands.review_followup: The PR is currently merge-conflicted and needs maintainer/contributor handling for the rebase plus a small fallback-path fix before normal merge review.

target.title: feat(gateway): managed outgoing attachments for documents (xlsx/pdf/docx) — Bug #9
target.author: adapepper

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: adapepper
commands.command: — addressing the codex review for `aafec535cb` (now `de1f5ea68b`). ## p2: preserve the original document filename — fixed > "savemediasource stores files under generated names, so the handler later > sends content-disposition with a uuid-style filename instead of the original > label; the pr's own real proof shows this for `pricing.xlsx`. please store > the derived original label/source filename here and add create-to-serve > coverage that downloads as `pricing.xlsx`." done in `de1f5ea68b`. the record now stores the derived `label` (the original filename) as the canonical download name; `torecordfilename(savedoriginal.path)` falls back only if `label` is empty. the on-disk path keeps its uuid basename for collision avoidance — this only changes what the browser's save as dialog shows. ```diff original: { path: savedoriginal.path, contenttype: savedcontenttype, sizebytes, - filename: torecordfilename(savedoriginal.path) ?? label, + // use the original derived label (e.g. pricing.xlsx) as the download + // filename rather than the saved-on-disk basename, which is a uuid + // (e.g. 2f09ba44-…-933f.xlsx) chosen by savemediasource for collision + // avoidance. the handler interpolates this into the + // `content-disposition: attachment; filename="…"` header, so the user + // sees the original filename in their browser's save as dialog. + filename: label || torecordfilename(savedoriginal.path) || "document", }, ``` ### create-to-serve regression coverage — added `it("create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through content-disposition (codex p2 regression for #77877)")` in `managed-document-attachments.test.ts`. real pk-prefixed xlsx → `createmanagedoutgoingdocumentblocks` → on-disk record json → `handlemanagedoutgoingdocumenthttprequest`. asserts both record-level (`original.filename === "pricing.xlsx"`) and wire-level (`content-disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"`). ``` $ pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.test.ts -t "create-to-serve" --reporter=verbose ✓ gateway-core handlemanagedoutgoingdocumenthttprequest — bug #9 > create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through content-disposition (codex p2 regression for #77877) 32ms ✓ gateway-server handlemanagedoutgoingdocumenthttprequest — bug #9 > create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through content-disposition (codex p2 regression for #77877) 32ms ✓ gateway-client handlemanagedoutgoingdocumenthttprequest — bug #9 > create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through content-disposition (codex p2 regression for #77877) 32ms test files 3 passed (3) tests 3 passed (3) | 51 skipped (54) ``` full suite: 54/54 pass (3 projects × 18 tests). ## p3: changelog entry — added ``` ### fixes - gateway/chat: serve assistant-generated outgoing document attachments (xlsx/pdf/docx/etc) through a dedicated managed-document http route with `content-disposition: attachment; filename="<original>"` so document downloads round-trip the original filename through the gateway, not the savemediasource uuid basename. (#77877) ``` ## real behavior proof — before/after with no mocks this drives the **patched module** end-to-end against a real pk-prefixed `.xlsx` fixture, with `openclaw_state_dir` pointed at a tmp dir, real `savemediasource`. no vitest, no mocks — the same harness runs against `main` to show the bug. ### after fix (`de1f5ea68b`) ``` $ npx tsx _77877_create_only.mjs returned block: [ { "type": "attachment", "attachment": { "url": "/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/agent%3abug9-proof%3asession%3acreate-only/30cd9fa5-1e74-459b-b844-63997d5b6e8e/full", "kind": "document", "label": "pricing.xlsx", "mimetype": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet" } } ] on-disk record: { "attachmentid": "30cd9fa5-1e74-459b-b844-63997d5b6e8e", "sessionkey": "agent:bug9-proof:session:create-only", "messageid": "msg-1", "createdat": "2026-05-07t19:56:44.069z", "retentionclass": "history", "label": "pricing.xlsx", "original": { "path": ".../15b53a8b-d40d-4387-b6a7-21c9e2e6344e.xlsx", "contenttype": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "sizebytes": 377, "filename": "pricing.xlsx" ← ← ← original filename, was uuid before fix } } verdict: rec.label === "pricing.xlsx" : ✓ (pricing.xlsx) rec.original.filename === "pricing.xlsx" : ✓ (pricing.xlsx) on-disk basename is uuid-ish != "pricing.xlsx" : ✓ (15b53a8b-d40d-4387-b6a7-21c9e2e6344e.xlsx) ``` ### before fix (same harness, `git stash` of the one-line change) ``` "original": { "path": ".../26301836-a398-492e-9952-70874882faad.xlsx", "contenttype": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "sizebytes": 377, "filename": "26301836-a398-492e-9952-70874882faad.xlsx" ← ← ← the bug codex flagged } verdict: rec.original.filename === "pricing.xlsx" : ✗ (26301836-a398-492e-9952-70874882faad.xlsx) ``` ### wire-level content-disposition (regression test output) ``` expect(result.headers["content-disposition"]).tobe('attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"'); ✓ pass ``` ## acceptance criteria | criterion | status | |---|---| | `pnpm test src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.test.ts` | ✓ 54/54 passing (was 51, now 54) | | `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.ts src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.test.ts changelog.md` | ✓ clean | | `pnpm tsgo:core` | clean on touched files | artifacts: `~/.openclaw/workspace/data/openclaw-pr-proofs/77877/`. cc @vincentkoc @steipete @bunsdev — please re-review.
commands.freeform_prompt: — addressing the Codex review for `aafec535cb` (now `de1f5ea68b`). ## P2: Preserve the original document filename — FIXED > "saveMediaSource stores files under generated names, so the handler later > sends Content-Disposition with a UUID-style filename instead of the original > label; the PR's own real proof shows this for `pricing.xlsx`. Please store > the derived original label/source filename here and add create-to-serve > coverage that downloads as `pricing.xlsx`." Done in `de1f5ea68b`. The record now stores the derived `label` (the original filename) as the canonical download name; `toRecordFilename(savedOriginal.path)` falls back only if `label` is empty. The on-disk path keeps its UUID basename for collision avoidance — this only changes what the browser's Save As dialog shows. ```diff original: { path: savedOriginal.path, contentType: savedContentType, sizeBytes, - filename: toRecordFilename(savedOriginal.path) ?? label, + // Use the original derived label (e.g. pricing.xlsx) as the download + // filename rather than the saved-on-disk basename, which is a UUID + // (e.g. 2f09ba44-…-933f.xlsx) chosen by saveMediaSource for collision + // avoidance. The handler interpolates this into the + // `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="…"` header, so the user + // sees the original filename in their browser's Save As dialog. + filename: label || toRecordFilename(savedOriginal.path) || "document", }, ``` ### Create-to-serve regression coverage — ADDED `it("create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through Content-Disposition (Codex P2 regression for #77877)")` in `managed-document-attachments.test.ts`. Real PK-prefixed xlsx → `createManagedOutgoingDocumentBlocks` → on-disk record JSON → `handleManagedOutgoingDocumentHttpRequest`. Asserts both record-level (`original.filename === "pricing.xlsx"`) and wire-level (`Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"`). ``` $ pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.test.ts -t "create-to-serve" --reporter=verbose ✓ gateway-core handleManagedOutgoingDocumentHttpRequest — Bug #9 > create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through Content-Disposition (Codex P2 regression for #77877) 32ms ✓ gateway-server handleManagedOutgoingDocumentHttpRequest — Bug #9 > create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through Content-Disposition (Codex P2 regression for #77877) 32ms ✓ gateway-client handleManagedOutgoingDocumentHttpRequest — Bug #9 > create-to-serve: preserves the original filename through Content-Disposition (Codex P2 regression for #77877) 32ms Test Files 3 passed (3) Tests 3 passed (3) | 51 skipped (54) ``` Full suite: 54/54 pass (3 projects × 18 tests). ## P3: CHANGELOG entry — ADDED ``` ### Fixes - Gateway/chat: serve assistant-generated outgoing document attachments (xlsx/pdf/docx/etc) through a dedicated managed-document HTTP route with `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<original>"` so document downloads round-trip the original filename through the gateway, not the saveMediaSource UUID basename. (#77877) ``` ## Real behavior proof — before/after with no mocks This drives the **patched module** end-to-end against a real PK-prefixed `.xlsx` fixture, with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` pointed at a tmp dir, real `saveMediaSource`. No vitest, no mocks — the same harness runs against `main` to show the bug. ### After fix (`de1f5ea68b`) ``` $ npx tsx _77877_create_only.mjs Returned block: [ { "type": "attachment", "attachment": { "url": "/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/agent%3Abug9-proof%3Asession%3Acreate-only/30cd9fa5-1e74-459b-b844-63997d5b6e8e/full", "kind": "document", "label": "pricing.xlsx", "mimeType": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet" } } ] On-disk record: { "attachmentId": "30cd9fa5-1e74-459b-b844-63997d5b6e8e", "sessionKey": "agent:bug9-proof:session:create-only", "messageId": "msg-1", "createdAt": "2026-05-07T19:56:44.069Z", "retentionClass": "history", "label": "pricing.xlsx", "original": { "path": ".../15b53a8b-d40d-4387-b6a7-21c9e2e6344e.xlsx", "contentType": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "sizeBytes": 377, "filename": "pricing.xlsx" ← ← ← original filename, was UUID before fix } } Verdict: rec.label === "pricing.xlsx" : ✓ (pricing.xlsx) rec.original.filename === "pricing.xlsx" : ✓ (pricing.xlsx) on-disk basename is UUID-ish != "pricing.xlsx" : ✓ (15b53a8b-d40d-4387-b6a7-21c9e2e6344e.xlsx) ``` ### Before fix (same harness, `git stash` of the one-line change) ``` "original": { "path": ".../26301836-a398-492e-9952-70874882faad.xlsx", "contentType": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "sizeBytes": 377, "filename": "26301836-a398-492e-9952-70874882faad.xlsx" ← ← ← the bug Codex flagged } Verdict: rec.original.filename === "pricing.xlsx" : ✗ (26301836-a398-492e-9952-70874882faad.xlsx) ``` ### Wire-level Content-Disposition (regression test output) ``` expect(result.headers["content-disposition"]).toBe('attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"'); ✓ PASS ``` ## Acceptance criteria | Criterion | Status | |---|---| | `pnpm test src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.test.ts` | ✓ 54/54 passing (was 51, now 54) | | `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.ts src/gateway/managed-document-attachments.test.ts CHANGELOG.md` | ✓ clean | | `pnpm tsgo:core` | clean on touched files | Artifacts: `~/.openclaw/workspace/data/openclaw-pr-proofs/77877/`. cc @vincentkoc @steipete @BunsDev — please re-review.
